economic_notes: Salt River First Nation is beneficiary of a Settlement Trust with fund balance of $72,908,048 at March 31, 2023 (Dec 31, 2022: $73,584,754). Trust distributions fund operations, infrastructure, per capita distributions, and youth/education. SRFN Development Corporation had total revenue of $8,068,499 and net loss of $804,392. Community subdivision under construction with carrying value of $16,958,102. Two homes on Whiperwill and Wabler Street under construction.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to failure to identify Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities under PS 3280 adopted April 1, 2022. Nation is in Northwest Territories (NT), not a province. Auditor firm name not clearly stated - only 'Chartered Professional Accountants' from Leduc, Alberta, dated April 14, 2025 (filed very late - over 2 years after year-end). Salt River First Nation Settlement Trust (~$72.9M) is EXCLUDED from consolidated financials. SRFN Development Corporation accounted for by modified equity method (GBE). Nation not in compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act (failed to post financials within 120 days). Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,417,735) + GNWT ($1,425,280) + FNIHB ($438,350) = $5,281,365. Net debt of ($4,029,074) per statement of change in net debt. tangible_capital_assets net book value taken from Schedule 1 as $40,066,301 (2023 net book value). expenses_infrastructure mapped to Lands/Public Works; expenses_social mapped to Child and Family Development; expenses_health mapped to Wellness.
